thesis: MasTec is a leading North American infrastructure contractor benefiting from secular tailwinds in grid modernization, broadband expansion, clean energy buildout, and data center electrification. Q1 2026 revenue grew 34% YoY to $3.83B with adjusted EBITDA of $284M (+73% YoY) and record backlog of $20.3B. FY2026 guidance raised to $17.5B revenue and $1.5B adjusted EBITDA. Valuation at ~62x trailing P/E and ~21x EV/EBITDA is elevated, requiring continued execution. The $3.0B debt load is manageable if operating cash flow exceeds $1B as guided. HOLD at current levels — wait for a pullback to $320–$340 for better entry.

financials.fcf: >$1B operating cash flow guided for FY2026

drivers: Infrastructure spending accelerates under federal programs (BEAD, IRA, grid hardening)
Margins expand to 10% as project mix improves and scale benefits kick in
Data center and clean energy backlog grows 40%+ annually
Operating cash flow exceeds $1B, enabling rapid deleveraging

risks: Infrastructure spending slows due to economic weakness or political gridlock
Tariffs and labor inflation compress margins back to 6–7%
Backlog burns without replacement; revenue growth drops to single digits
Debt covenants tighten; equity dilution risk emerges

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows 15% annually through 2028 on backlog execution. EBITDA margins stabilize at 8.5–9.0%. Debt is managed via operating cash flow. No equity raise needed.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings release — watch margin trajectory and backlog update
Potential data center or clean energy contract announcements

key_risks: Project execution risk on large fixed-price contracts
$3.0B debt load is material if EBITDA growth stalls
Labor availability and wage inflation are persistent cost pressures
Tariffs on steel and equipment directly impact material costs
Valuation at 62x trailing P/E leaves no room for error
